# UC-22.8.21 · SOC 2 CC7 — Incident Classification Consistency and Severity Drift Audit

## Description

CC7 expects defined incident response categories. Tracking reclassification events (severity lowered post-facto, category changes near month-end) supports integrity of incident metrics used for management reporting and customer notifications.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:incident, snow:incident_audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Ensure audit subflow indexes to Splunk; (2) map numeric severity scale; (3) route downgrades to IR quality review; (4) correlate with customer breach notification timestamps; (5) quarterly governance review of outliers. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:incident`, `sourcetype=snow:incident_audit`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, severity, category, sys_updated_on); `snow:incident_audit` or `sys_audit` replica with field changes.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure audit subflow indexes to Splunk; (2) map numeric severity scale; (3) route downgrades to IR quality review; (4) correlate with customer breach notification timestamps; (5) quarterly governance review of outliers.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident_audit" field_name IN ("severity","category") earliest=-90d
| streamstats window=1 global=f latest(new_value) as prev_val by number, field_name
| where isnotnull(prev_val) AND new_value!=prev_val
| eval downgrade=if(field_name="severity" AND tonumber(new_value)>tonumber(prev_val),1,0)
| stats count as changes, sum(downgrade) as severity_downgrades by number
| where severity_downgrades>0 OR changes>3
| sort - changes
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC7 — Incident Classification Consistency and Severity Drift Audit** — CC7 expects defined incident response categories. Tracking reclassification events (severity lowered post-facto, category changes near month-end) supports integrity of incident metrics used for management reporting and customer notifications.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, severity, category, sys_updated_on); `snow:incident_audit` or `sys_audit` replica with field changes. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident_audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:incident_audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by number, field_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(prev_val) AND new_value!=prev_val` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **downgrade** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by number** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where severity_downgrades>0 OR changes>3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (incidents), Bar chart (downgrades by month), Single value (distinct incidents).

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident_audit" field_name IN ("severity","category") earliest=-90d
| streamstats window=1 global=f latest(new_value) as prev_val by number, field_name
| where isnotnull(prev_val) AND new_value!=prev_val
| eval downgrade=if(field_name="severity" AND tonumber(new_value)>tonumber(prev_val),1,0)
| stats count as changes, sum(downgrade) as severity_downgrades by number
| where severity_downgrades>0 OR changes>3
| sort - changes
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (incidents), Bar chart (downgrades by month), Single value (distinct incidents).
